req.title: System-Admin updates policy-document
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a policy-document through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```python
"""
Tests for Policy Document Update API
Requirement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a policy-document
through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must
be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, ANY
from datetime import datetime, timezone
import json


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_token(client):
    """Return a valid JWT / session token for a system-admin user."""
    response = client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"username": "sys_admin", "password": "S3cur3P@ss!"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    return response.json()["access_token"]


@pytest.fixture
def regular_user_token(client):
    """Return a valid token for a non-admin user."""
    response = client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"username": "regular_user", "password": "UserP@ss!"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    return response.json()["access_token"]


@pytest.fixture
def existing_policy(client, system_admin_token):
    """Create and return a policy document to be used in update tests."""
    payload = {
        "title": "Initial Policy Title",
        "content": "Initial policy content.",
        "version": "1.0",
        "effective_date": "2024-01-01",
        "category": "security",
    }
    response = client.post(
        "/api/policy-documents",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {system_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 201
    return response.json()


@pytest.fixture
def valid_update_payload():
    """Return a complete, valid update payload."""
    return {
        "title": "Updated Policy Title",
        "content": "Updated policy content.",
        "version": "1.1",
        "effective_date": "2024-06-01",
        "category": "security",
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

POLICY_ENDPOINT = "/api/policy-documents/{policy_id}"


def update_url(policy_id):
    return POLICY_ENDPOINT.format(policy_id=policy_id)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Authentication & Authorisation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAuthentication:
    def test_update_without_token_returns_401(self, client, existing_policy, valid_update_payload):
        """Unauthenticated requests must be rejected with 401."""
        response = client.put(
            update_url(existing_policy["id"]),
            json=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert response.status_code == 401

    def test_update_with_invalid_token_returns_401(self, client, existing_policy, valid_update_payload):
        """Requests with an invalid/expired token must be rejected with 401."""
        response = client.put(
            update_url(existing_policy["id"]),
            json=valid_update_payload,
            headers={"Authorization": "Bearer invalid.token.here"},
        )
        assert response.status_code == 401

    def test_update_with_non_admin_role_returns_403(
        self, client, existing_policy, valid_update_payload, regular_user_token
    ):
        """Non-admin authenticated users must be rejected with 403."""
        response = client.put(
            update_url(existing_policy["id"]),
            json=valid_update_payload,
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {regular_user_token}"},
        )
        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_update_with_system_admin_role_succeeds(
        self, client, existing_policy, valid_update_payload, system_admin_token
    ):
        """A system-admin with a valid token must be permitted to update."""
